message:
fix(pager): allow reading pages allocated by current transaction

The previous commit (4202ea30) added an MVCC snapshot db_size guard that
correctly rejects reads of pages beyond the transaction's snapshot boundary.
However, it was too strict — it also rejected reads of pages that THIS
transaction allocated but hadn't written yet.

This breaks normal B-tree operations:
1. Transaction starts with published_db_size=100
2. B-tree split allocates page 101 (added to allocated_from_eof)
3. Transaction tries to read page 101 to initialize B-tree content
4. REJECTED because 101 > 100 → crash

Fix:
- Before returning BusySnapshot, check if the page is in:
  - allocated_from_eof (pages allocated from beyond current db size)
  - allocated_from_freelist (pages recycled from freelist)
  - page_lease (pre-allocated page batch for reduced lock contention)
- If the page was allocated by THIS transaction, allow the read
- Only reject reads of pages allocated by OTHER transactions

Also: update published_db_size after sub-transaction (savepoint) commits.
When a savepoint releases, the main transaction inherits its db_size
changes, so the MVCC boundary must advance accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
